name: Ableton Live
description: Music production DAW built around a unique session/arrangement duality. Industry standard for electronic music and live performance.

name: FL Studio
description: Popular DAW with a pattern-based workflow, lifetime free updates, and a huge plugin ecosystem.

name: Logic Pro
description: Apple's professional DAW. Deep MIDI editing, huge sound library, and tight macOS integration.

name: Reaper
description: Lightweight, affordable DAW with extreme customizability and broad format/plugin support.

name: Bitwig Studio
description: Modern DAW with a modular sound engine, hardware integration, and flexible clip launching.

name: Audacity
description: Free, open-source audio editor for recording and editing. Simple but capable for podcasts and basic mixing.

name: GarageBand
description: Apple's free music creation app. Beginner-friendly with loops, virtual instruments, and lessons.

name: LMMS
description: Free, open-source DAW for beat production and electronic music. Built-in synthesizers, samples, and VST support.

name: Ardour
description: Free, open-source DAW for recording, editing, and mixing audio. Full MIDI support and professional-grade routing.

name: MuseScore
description: Free, open-source music notation software. Write, play back, and print sheet music with a full WYSIWYG editor.

name: DaVinci Resolve
description: Professional video editor with industry-leading color grading, Fusion VFX, and Fairlight audio — all free.

name: Adobe Premiere Pro
description: Industry-standard video editor for film, TV, and web. Deep integration with After Effects and Adobe ecosystem.

name: Final Cut Pro
description: Apple's professional video editor. Magnetic timeline, optimized for Apple silicon, ProRes support.

name: Kdenlive
description: Free, open-source video editor by KDE. Multi-track editing, effects, and titling. Strong Linux support.

name: Shotcut
description: Free, open-source cross-platform video editor with a wide format support and no import required.

name: CapCut
description: Free video editor by ByteDance. Popular for short-form content with templates, effects, and auto-captions.

name: OpenShot
description: Free, open-source video editor with a simple drag-and-drop interface, 3D animations, and keyframe support.

name: Wondershare Filmora
description: Beginner-friendly video editor with drag-and-drop effects, transitions, and stock media. Quick results for casual editors.

name: iMovie
description: Apple's free video editor for beginners. Trailers, themes, and easy timeline editing on Mac and iOS.

name: Lightworks
description: Professional NLE with a free tier. Used on Hollywood films like Pulp Fiction and The King's Speech.

name: Olive
description: Free, open-source non-linear video editor in alpha. GPU-accelerated rendering and node-based compositing.

name: LosslessCut
description: Fast, lossless video/audio trimming and cutting tool built on FFmpeg. No re-encoding required.

name: Movavi Video Editor
description: Consumer video editor with drag-and-drop timeline, transitions, filters, and AI-powered features like background removal.

name: Pitivi
description: Free, open-source video editor for Linux built on GStreamer. Clean interface focused on simplicity.

name: Flowblade
description: Free, open-source multitrack non-linear video editor for Linux. Fast workflow with G'MIC filtering support.

name: Adobe Photoshop
description: The image editor. Raster graphics, photo manipulation, compositing, digital painting — the industry standard.

name: GIMP
description: Free, open-source image editor. Feature-rich alternative to Photoshop with scripting and plugin support.

name: Affinity Photo
description: Professional photo editor now part of the free unified Affinity app. RAW editing, HDR merge, focus stacking, and more.

name: Photopea
description: Free browser-based image editor that opens PSD, XCF, Sketch files. No install needed.

name: Paint.NET
description: Free Windows image editor. Layers, effects, and plugins. Much more capable than MS Paint.

name: Krita
description: Free, open-source digital painting app. Brush engines, layer management, and animation tools built for artists.

name: Pixelmator Pro
description: Mac-native image editor with ML-powered tools for photo editing, design, and painting. One-time purchase.

name: Pixlr
description: Browser-based image editor with AI-powered tools, layers, and effects. Quick edits without installing software.

name: RawTherapee
description: Free, open-source RAW photo processing program. Non-destructive editing with advanced color management and batch processing.

name: Canva
description: Browser-based design platform with templates for social media, presentations, and print. Drag-and-drop with a huge asset library.

name: Luminar Neo
description: AI-powered photo editor by Skylum. One-click sky replacement, portrait retouching, and creative effects.

name: Acorn
description: Image editor for macOS with layers, filters, vector tools, and a non-destructive workflow. Lighter than Photoshop.

name: Adobe Lightroom
description: Photo editor and organizer for managing large libraries. Non-destructive RAW editing, presets, and cloud sync.

name: Darktable
description: Free, open-source photography workflow app. Non-destructive RAW processing, tethered shooting, and advanced color management.

name: digiKam
description: Free, open-source photo management app with advanced tagging, face recognition, and geotagging for large collections.

name: Google Photos
description: Cloud photo storage and organization with AI-powered search, automatic albums, and built-in editing tools.

name: Immich
description: Self-hosted photo and video backup solution with mobile upload, ML-based search, and a Google Photos-like UI.

name: PhotoPrism
description: Self-hosted, AI-powered photo management app. Automatic tagging, face recognition, and map view for geotagged photos.

name: Apple Photos
description: Apple's built-in photo library manager. iCloud sync, Memories, face/object recognition, and basic editing tools.

name: Shotwell
description: Simple photo manager for GNOME. Import, organize, tag, and do basic edits on your local photo library.

name: Piwigo
description: Self-hosted photo gallery for the web. Organize thousands of photos with albums, tags, and user management.

name: Lychee
description: Free, self-hosted photo management tool. Beautiful gallery UI with albums, tags, and sharing built in.

name: Adobe Illustrator
description: Industry-standard vector graphics editor for logos, icons, illustrations, and print design.

name: Inkscape
description: Free, open-source vector graphics editor. SVG-native with path editing, clones, filters, and extensions.

name: Affinity Designer
description: Vector and raster design tool with pixel-perfect precision. Now part of the free unified Affinity app by Canva.

name: Linearity Curve
description: Mac and iOS vector design app (formerly Vectornator). Intuitive pen tool, auto-trace, and real-time collaboration.

name: Vectr
description: Free, browser-based vector graphics editor for creating simple vector art, logos, and presentations.

name: SVG-Edit
description: Free, open-source browser-based SVG editor. Lightweight tool for creating and editing SVG files directly in the browser.

name: Boxy SVG
description: Modern SVG editor for web designers. Clean interface with precise control over SVG elements and CSS styling.

name: Gravit Designer
description: Free vector design app available on all platforms. Full-featured with path editing, pages, and export to SVG, PDF, and PNG.

name: VectorStyler
description: Advanced vector graphics editor with multi-artboard support, parametric shapes, and extensive typography controls.

name: Blender
description: Free, open-source 3D creation suite. Modeling, sculpting, animation, rendering, compositing, and video editing.

name: Autodesk Maya
description: Industry-standard 3D software for film VFX, game assets, and animation. Extremely deep toolset.

name: Cinema 4D
description: Professional 3D software known for motion graphics, ease of use, and After Effects integration.

name: FreeCAD
description: Free, open-source parametric 3D CAD modeler for engineering and product design.

name: SketchUp
description: Intuitive 3D modeling tool popular for architecture, interior design, and woodworking. Easy to learn.

name: Fusion 360
description: Cloud-based CAD/CAM/CAE by Autodesk. Parametric modeling, simulation, and manufacturing in one tool.

name: OpenSCAD
description: Free, open-source script-based 3D CAD modeler. Define models with code rather than interactive modeling.

name: Rhino
description: NURBS-based 3D modeling for industrial design, architecture, and jewelry. Extensible with Grasshopper visual scripting.

name: Houdini
description: Procedural 3D software for VFX, simulations, and motion graphics. Node-based workflow used in film and games.

name: Tinkercad
description: Free, browser-based 3D modeling tool by Autodesk. Beginner-friendly for 3D printing, electronics, and coding.

name: SolidWorks
description: Professional CAD software for mechanical engineering and product design. Parametric modeling, simulation, and PDM.

name: Onshape
description: Cloud-native CAD platform with real-time collaboration. Parametric modeling, assemblies, and version control in the browser.

name: Plasticity
description: Fast, production-ready 3D modeling tool for concept artists and designers. NURBS-based with a minimal, focused UI.

name: VLC
description: Plays everything. Free, open-source media player that handles virtually any audio/video format.

name: mpv
description: Minimalist, high-quality media player. Scriptable, GPU-accelerated, and beloved by power users.

name: IINA
description: Modern macOS media player built on mpv. Native UI, picture-in-picture, and Touch Bar support.

name: foobar2000
description: Highly customizable audio player for Windows. Lightweight, supports advanced tagging and DSP.

name: Spotify
description: Dominant music streaming service with 100M+ tracks, podcasts, playlists, and social features.

name: Plex
description: Media server that organizes and streams your personal video, music, and photo libraries to any device.

name: MusicBee
description: Feature-rich music manager and player for Windows. Great library management, skins, and plugin support.

name: Jellyfin
description: Free, open-source media server. Self-hosted Plex alternative with no premium tier — all features are free.

name: Kodi
description: Free, open-source media center. Plays local and network media with add-ons, skins, and PVR support.

name: Strawberry Music Player
description: Free, open-source music player and library organizer. Fork of Clementine with audio CD support and tag editing.

name: OBS Studio
description: Free, open-source software for live streaming and screen recording. The standard for streamers and creators.

name: Streamlabs
description: Streaming platform built on OBS with alerts, overlays, widgets, and an integrated app store.

name: Loom
description: Quick video messaging for work. Record screen and camera, share with a link, get viewer analytics.

name: ShareX
description: Free, open-source screen capture and recording tool for Windows. GIF capture, annotations, and auto-upload.

name: Camtasia
description: Screen recorder and video editor for creating tutorials and presentations. Templates and annotations built in.

name: ScreenPal
description: Simple screen recorder and editor with webcam overlay, annotations, and easy sharing.

name: Flameshot
description: Free, open-source screenshot tool with annotation, blur, and upload features. Popular on Linux desktops.

name: Kap
description: Free, open-source screen recorder for macOS. Exports to GIF, MP4, WebM, and APNG with plugin support.

name: Pillow
description: Python imaging library fork. Open, manipulate, and save image files in dozens of formats.

name: Sharp
description: High-performance Node.js image processing. Resize, convert, and transform images using libvips. Very fast.

name: ImageMagick
description: CLI suite for image conversion and manipulation. Resize, crop, composite, and convert between 200+ formats.

name: OpenCV
description: Open-source computer vision library. Image processing, object detection, video analysis, and ML modules.

name: libvips
description: Fast image processing library with low memory usage. Handles large images efficiently with streaming pipelines.

name: ExifTool
description: CLI tool for reading, writing, and editing metadata in image, audio, and video files. Supports 400+ formats.

name: GraphicsMagick
description: Image processing suite forked from ImageMagick. Focuses on stability, performance, and a smaller attack surface.

name: Squoosh
description: Browser-based image compression tool by Google. Compare codecs side-by-side and optimize images with real-time preview.

name: TinyPNG
description: Smart lossy compression for PNG and JPEG images. Reduces file sizes significantly while preserving visual quality.

name: imgproxy
description: Fast, secure image processing server. Resize, crop, and convert images on-the-fly via URL parameters.

name: Tesseract OCR
description: Open-source OCR engine maintained by Google. Extracts text from images in 100+ languages with high accuracy.

name: pngquant
description: Lossy PNG compression tool. Reduces file sizes significantly while preserving transparency and visual quality.

name: SVGO
description: Node.js tool for optimizing SVG files. Removes unnecessary metadata, comments, and redundant attributes to shrink file size.

name: scikit-image
description: Python image processing library built on NumPy. Filtering, segmentation, feature detection, and morphology algorithms.

name: Upscayl
description: Free, open-source AI image upscaler. Enhance resolution up to 16x on desktop with multiple upscaling models.

name: Thumbor
description: Smart image processing HTTP server. On-demand cropping, resizing, filters, and AI-powered face detection for thumbnails.

name: GStreamer
description: Pipeline-based multimedia framework for streaming, editing, and processing audio and video. Modular plugin architecture.

name: FFmpeg
description: Swiss army knife of audio/video processing. Transcode, stream, filter, and mux virtually any media format.

name: HandBrake
description: Free, open-source video transcoder for converting video between formats. Presets for common devices and quality levels.

name: yt-dlp
description: CLI tool for downloading video and audio from YouTube and thousands of other sites. Fork of youtube-dl with active development.

name: MediaInfo
description: Displays technical metadata for audio and video files. Shows codec, bitrate, resolution, and container details.

name: SoX
description: CLI audio processing tool for converting, recording, and applying effects to audio files. The Swiss army knife of audio.

name: MKVToolNix
description: Tools for creating, altering, and inspecting Matroska (MKV) files. Merge, split, and edit tracks and metadata.

name: Avidemux
description: Free video editor for simple cutting, filtering, and encoding tasks. Supports AVI, MP4, MKV, and many codecs.

name: Mp3tag
description: Powerful audio metadata editor for Windows. Batch-edit tags, rename files, and fetch cover art for MP3, FLAC, and more.

name: Shutter Encoder
description: Free media converter based on FFmpeg. Convert video, audio, and images with a visual interface and batch processing.
